title: Global well-posedness for the 2D one-phase Muskat equations
speaker: Hongjie Dong
abstract: I will present a recent result jointly with Francisco Gancedo (Universidad de Sevilla) and Huy Q. Nguyen (University of Maryland), in which we obtained the global well-posedness for the 2D one-phase Muskat problem with arbitrary large Lipschitz initial data. The equation arises from a free boundary problem for a 2D fluid filtered in porous media.

proof_stages.label: Reduction to a graph Dirichlet-to-Neumann evolution
proof_stages.summary: The Muskat free-boundary problem is specialized to the one-phase/vacuum case, Darcy's law and incompressibility reduce the pressure potential to a harmonic Dirichlet problem, and the graph interface equation is written as a nonlocal evolution involving the Dirichlet-to-Neumann operator.

proof_stages.label: Critical Lipschitz setting and global theorem
proof_stages.summary: Scaling identifies Lipschitz regularity as critical, and the main result is stated: arbitrary periodic Lipschitz graph data, with no smallness assumption, generate a unique global viscosity solution with a uniform Lipschitz bound.

proof_stages.label: Viscosity sub- and supersolution framework
proof_stages.summary: The nonlocal equation is interpreted in viscosity form by testing upper or lower semicontinuous functions with smooth functions touching from above or below and imposing the corresponding Dirichlet-to-Neumann inequality at the contact point.

proof_stages.label: Parabolic regularization scheme
proof_stages.summary: Existence is approached through a regularized equation adding an epsilon multiple of the second spatial derivative, producing approximate smooth solutions whose limit should solve the original Muskat equation.

proof_stages.label: Layer-potential representation of the Dirichlet-to-Neumann map
proof_stages.summary: In the two-dimensional periodic geometry, the explicit Green function and double-layer potential theory on Lipschitz domains give an explicit representation of the Dirichlet-to-Neumann operator and quantitative bounds in terms of the Lipschitz norm of the interface.

proof_stages.label: Uniform estimates and passage to a global solution
proof_stages.summary: The layer-potential bounds yield a priori estimates for the regularized solutions in L2, H1, and H2-type norms, which provide the compactness and stability needed to pass to a viscosity solution of the original equation.

proof_stages.label: Consistency via pointwise boundary regularity
proof_stages.summary: A viscosity solution that is C^{1,1} at a point is shown to satisfy the equation classically there, relying on a pointwise C^{1,alpha} estimate for harmonic functions vanishing on a Lipschitz boundary that is C^{1,1} at the point, proved by flat approximation, W^{1,p} estimates, and iteration.

proof_stages.label: Comparison principle and uniqueness
proof_stages.summary: Using the consistency result together with Ishii-type convolution arguments, viscosity subsolutions and supersolutions satisfy a comparison principle, which implies uniqueness and explains the nonincrease of the Lipschitz norm.
